'''Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragons''' were huge, violent beasts that were introduced to the populated worlds of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by [[Nomad-Damon]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] beasts were utilized as mounts by some individuals. With the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], however, the War Dragons were largely wiped out.

==Biology and appearance==
Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragons were large beasts, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] in appearance. They were covered in horns, on their head and shoulders, as well as their feet and tail. On the head of the War Dragons in particular, there were six large horns—four facing backward and two facing forward, just under the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. An additional two horns were found on their hips. The War Dragons could be used as mounts, with a prospective rider sitting just behind the front [[Limb/Legends|legs]] of the beast. The skin of the War Dragons was largely green, although its back was covered in dark mottled spots, and its underbelly possessed a distinctly lighter coloring.<ref name="daa" />

==Behavior==
Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragons were noted for their violent temperaments.<ref name="daa" />

==History==
The Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragons were introduced to the populated worlds of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="daa" /> a [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="rots">{{Film|III}}</ref> The arbiter of that effort was an individual known as [[Nomad-Damon]], a traveler. Members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] attempted to tame the violent beasts, but their efforts were largely in vain.<ref name="daa" /> At the conclusion of the Clone Wars, the Republic gave way to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="rots" /> and under the new rule the War Dragons were destroyed by agents of the government. Any survivors of the Imperial purges disappeared, or were sold off to wealthy civilians. At some point, an [[War Dragon rider|individual]], wielding a modified [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], used a War Dragon as a mount and chose to sling a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] on his beast's neck.<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
In [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition was announced, inviting readers to design ''[[Star Wars]]'' aliens inspired and influenced by the [[original trilogy]] of films. 29 year old [[Mark Rehkopf]] designed the Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragon, submitting his artwork to the magazine for the competition. Rehkopf's entry won first place "16 years and up" division, and his blurb for the creature, as well as his original artwork, was published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]). [[Leland Chee]], the [[Lucasfilm]] continuity editor, later stated the "Design an Alien" material is [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the "Design an Alien" contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> In spite of this, the Ubese Thorn-Back War Dragon has not been referenced in any other ''Star Wars'' works.
